honest-gherkin/src/honest_gherkin/ir.py:: TypedDict total= is a static-only annotation; at runtime total=True and total=False produce an identical class whose instances are plain dicts with no required-key enforcement. parse builds every Step with all four keys, so no constructible input distinguishes the two.
honest-gherkin/src/honest_gherkin/cli.py: dest="command"->"" stores the chosen subcommand under the "" attribute; main reads only args.path and args.steps, never args.command, so no behaviour changes.
prog="honest-gherkin"->"" affects only argparse usage/help/error text; main reads no prog and the exit codes and reports are unchanged.
the emptied string is the subparser help text (the "run" name itself is caught); help affects only --help output, not parsing or exit codes.
the emptied string is the argument help text (the "path" dest is caught); help affects only --help output.
the emptied string is the argument help text (the "--steps" flag is caught); help affects only --help output.
if __name__ == "" is never true under import (name is honest_gherkin.cli) nor as a script (name is __main__), so the guarded block never runs; unreachable under test, equivalent.
removes the __main__ guard block, which only runs when cli.py is executed as a script; under import the block never runs, so behaviour is identical.
replaces the __main__ guard body with pass; the guard is False under import so neither the original raise nor pass ever executes — equivalent.
honest-gherkin/src/honest_gherkin/regist: matches[0] is reached only when exactly one pattern matched (count==0 returns step_unmatched and count>1 returns ambiguous_step before this line), so matches has one element and matches[0]==matches[-1]. Verified identical on one-match, zero-match, and ambiguous inputs.
_flush resets current to None only to avoid re-appending the same scenario; _on_scenario always overwrites current immediately after its flush, and the end-of-parse flush is the last operation, so a non-reset current is never flushed again. Verified identical across the valid-input battery.

_on_feature re-setting in_header=True is redundant: on the sole Feature line in_header is already True from the initial state. Only a second Feature line followed by a description — outside the one-Feature-per-file grammar — observes it. Verified identical on all valid inputs; differs only on a 2nd-Feature input.

Initial state in_header key emptied: in_header is read only by _on_description, and on valid input the Feature line (which sets in_header) precedes any description, so the initial key is overwritten before it is read. Only a description before the Feature line — malformed — observes it. Verified identical on all valid inputs.

Initial state in_header=False: the first Feature line sets in_header=True before any description line, so the initial value is overwritten before it is read. Only a description preceding the Feature line — malformed — observes it. Verified identical on all valid inputs; differs only on a pre-Feature description.

Initial state in_header key swapped with a sibling: in_header is created by the first Feature line before any description reads it, so the initial mapping is overwritten on valid input. Only a pre-Feature description — malformed — observes it. Verified identical on all valid inputs.

Initial state last_resolved_kind key emptied: last_resolved_kind is read only by _on_step, which runs only when a current scenario exists, which _on_scenario establishes while setting last_resolved_kind. The initial value is therefore overwritten before it can be read. Verified identical across the valid-input battery.

Initial state last_resolved_kind key swapped with a sibling: read only by _on_step under a current scenario, which _on_scenario establishes while setting last_resolved_kind, so the initial mapping is overwritten before it is read. Verified identical across the valid-input battery.
